subject: A skeletal undead crow with tattered wings perched amid bare, gnarled branches in a shadowy dead forest
description: A gaunt zombie bird stands on a thin, twisted branch in the foreground, its body skeletal and worn, with ragged dark feathers clinging to exposed bone. Its head tilts slightly, beak parted, eye glinting pale against the gloom. Around it a dense lattice of leafless, claw-like branches reaches in every direction, forming a near-monochromatic tangle of grey-black silhouettes. The background dissolves into murky greens and inky blacks, suggesting a decaying swamp or haunted forest under no visible sky. The atmosphere is hushed, oppressive, and funereal — the painting reads almost as an ink wash, with only faint highlights picking out the bird's ribs and the edge of a broken bough.
foreground: skeletal undead crow perched on a bare, twisted branch
background: tangled web of leafless dead branches fading into murky darkness
emotion: watchful, ominous stillness — a silent omen
